title: papaver rhoeas var. lecoqii
intro: Papaver rhoeas var. lecoqii, also known as the Shirley poppy, is a subspecies of the common poppy (Papaver rhoeas) native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Europe. It is a flowering plant with medicinal properties, primarily containing alkaloids such as protopine and sanguinarine, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Papaver rhoeas var. lecoqii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant, often in the form of extracts or tinctures, are used to treat colds, coughs, and other respiratory issues, although it is essential to use caution and consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
